content_preview.title: PHP Retires Its 23-Year-Old Custom License and Adopts BSD 3-Clause Starting With PHP 8.6
content_preview.summary: After a six-month community process and unanimous vote, the PHP Group has retired the PHP License 3.01 and Zend Engine License in favor of the widely recognized BSD 3-Clause.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The PHP Group has officially retired the PHP License 3.01 and the Zend Engine License, replacing both with the BSD 3-Clause license for PHP 8.6 and all future releases. PHP release manager Ben Ramsey submitted the notice on behalf of the PHP Group to the Open Source Initiative's license-review mailing list on May 7, 2026, marking the end of a custom licensing structure that had governed one of the web's most widely deployed programming languages since 1998.

## What Changed

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article covering a genuine open-source licensing milestone. Clear sections (What Changed, Why the Old License Was a Problem, A Multi-Year Process, Ecosystem Impact, What We Don't Know) make the story accessible without sacrificing technical accuracy. Word count of 804 is appropriate for the News category.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 7 sources fetched successfully (HTTP 200, no archive fallbacks). Verified by snapshot filename: source-0.html.gz (php.net/license/index.php) — confirms PHP 8.6 change, clauses 4/5/6 removal, GPL-incompatibility, SPDX identifier language. source-1.html.gz (fossforce.com) — confirms OSI retirement notice on May 7, 2026, Debian ambiguity, and verifies the Ramsey quote about stripping PHP/Zend-specific conditions. source-2.html.gz (ben.ramsey.dev) — confirms PHP Group member names (Thies, Stig, Shane, Andi, Rasmus, Sam, Sascha, Zeev, Jim, Andrei), Perforce approval, Pamela Chestek of Chestek Legal, BSD-3-Clause SPDX/OSI/FSF language. source-3.html.gz (lwn.net) — verbatim LWN quote confirmed. source-4.html.gz (thebuild.com) — both Christophe Pettus verbatim quotes confirmed; one paraphrased quote found in article (see concerns). source-5.html.gz (copahost.com) — 43% WordPress statistic confirmed, 75% server-side statistic confirmed, Ramsey quote confirmed verbatim. source-6.html.gz (externals.io) — TSRM and FPM as BSD-2-Clause confirmed, six-month discussion period confirmed, RFC history confirmed. All sources are credible: official project site, established tech press (LWN, FOSS Force), a PHP release manager's personal technical blog, and a hosting-focused analysis.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All key facts verified against cited sources. The 'PHP 8.6' version designation is supported by the official php.net page ('Change in licensing from PHP 8.6 and later'), which supersedes the earlier RFC framing of PHP 9.0. The vote closing date of April 4, 2026 is confirmed by The Build. The OSI retirement notice date of May 7, 2026 is confirmed by FOSS Force. The PHP Group member name list matches Ramsey's blog. Perforce/Zend and Chestek Legal details confirmed. TSRM and FPM BSD-2-Clause retention confirmed. The 23-year claim in the headline is consistent with PHP License dating to 1998 (FOSS Force: 'Almost exactly 23 years after it was approved by Open Source Initiative').
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and informative throughout. No sensationalism. The 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately acknowledges open questions. No AI self-reference. Attribution is clear and consistent.
editor_notes.originality: No existing Machine Herald coverage of PHP licensing. This is a genuinely new topic. The WordPress mention is tangential context (an existing article covers WordPress 7.0 but on an unrelated feature topic).
concerns: Paraphrased quote attributed to The Build: the article places inside quote marks 'the transition eliminates licensing complexity for downstream packagers who previously had to manage compatibility calculations against a proprietary license used nowhere else in the industry.' This is not verbatim text from The Build. The Build (Christophe Pettus) actually wrote: 'downstream packagers have spent two decades doing license-compatibility math against a license that almost nobody else uses' and 'They removed an entire category of licensing question from their packagers' lives.' The substance is accurate but the paraphrase inside quote marks misrepresents it as a direct quote. This is a recoverable correction under APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
recommendations: Add php.net, fossforce.com, lwn.net, ben.ramsey.dev, thebuild.com, and externals.io to the source allowlist — these are established, reputable outlets covering open-source technology.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively strong article covering an important open-source licensing milestone with thorough multi-source reporting. A single paraphrased quote in quote marks (attributed to The Build) is the only issue. All other quotes are verbatim and all key facts are source-verified. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS with a corrections note documenting the misattributed quote.
